title: Executive Order 11808—Establishing the President's Economic Policy Board, and for Other Purposes

flags.justification: While the full Board is broad (nearly the entire economic cabinet), Sec. 4's smaller Executive Committee meets daily and the Chairman is designated 'principal spokesman for the Executive Branch on matters of economic policy' (Sec. 3) — real operational concentration in a smaller group despite the nominally broad full membership.
No reporting requirement or review mechanism is specified for the Board's or Executive Committee's exercise of its 'oversee all economic policy' mandate.
Comprehensive scope across one coherent domain (economic policy coordination), not a bundling of unrelated matters.
'Oversee the formulation, coordination, and implementation of all economic policy' (Sec. 3) is extremely broad with no defined limits on what concrete actions this authorizes.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing coordination body for an ongoing government function, evergreen by nature, similar to other standing coordination structures in this corpus.

Sec. 6 carefully amends several existing bodies (Council on Wage and Price Stability, Council on International Economic Policy, National Advisory Council, Committee on East-West Trade) rather than dissolving them, preserving continuity.
No external check exists on this internal-executive-branch body's exercise of its broad economic-policy mandate.

A significant, novel peacetime economic-policy-coordination superstructure, comparable in ambition to wartime mobilization coordination bodies but explicitly designed for ongoing peacetime governance.
Sec. 6 deliberately and explicitly restructures four existing bodies with specific role assignments, rather than creating ad hoc overlap.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 5/20 = 25.0%. Ford's first major economic-governance reorganization, roughly seven weeks into his presidency.
